title: NO SPOILERS Eva 30 DVD BD Why the wait
display_title: NO SPOILERS Eva 30 DVD BD Why the wait
wikitext:
[]: [NO SPOILERS] Eva 3.0 DVD/BD - Why the wait?             Quote Post by IDontWantNoScrub » Sun Nov 25, 2012 8:01 am because in Japan all the Rebuild DVDs have come out in the month of May (1.01, 1.11 & 2.22). Or would that be too early this time, because 3.0 came out later in the year (compared to 1.0 & 2.0)? Edited the title (I don't kknow anything, just speculating).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 25, 2012 4:00 pm Patience, patience! When it's announced, the news will bounce straight to this place.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Nov 25, 2012 6:12 pm Probably Summer 2013 the earliest, I'd say.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Mon Nov 26, 2012 12:11 pm Here's a history of Evangelion movies (listing both their theatrical debuts and initial home video releases): Death and Rebirth 03/15/1997 theatrical debut 12/23/1998 VHS/LD release (Theatrical Edition box set with Death(True)² replacing original version of Death, also w/ EoE) The End of Evangelion 07/19/1997 theatrical debut 08/12/1998 VHS/LD release of 25' Air (on Genesis 0:13 w/ TV Ep. 25) 09/09/1998 VHS/LD release of 26' Magokoro wo Kimi ni (on Genesis 0:14 w/ TV Ep. 26) 12/23/1998 VHS/LD release of Theatrical Edition (box set with D+R) Revival of Evangelion (theatrical re-release of Death(True)², Air, and Magokoro wo Kimi ni) 03/15/1998 theatrical debut 12/23/1998 VHS/LD release of Theatrical Edition box set (included "Rebirth" as well) Evangelion 1.0 09/01/2007 theatrical debut 04/25/2008 LE DVD release of 1.01 Evangelion 2.0 06/27/2009 theatrical debut 05/26/2010 DVD/BD release of 2.22 Evangelion 3.0 11/17/2012 theatrical debut ??/??/2013 DVD/BD release of 3.33 Based on historical patterns, I'd say Autumn 2013 would be about right. If I had to bet, it would be either September or October...

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Mon Nov 26, 2012 12:41 pm svenge wrote: Evangelion 1.0 09/01/2007 theatrical debut 04/25/2008 LE DVD release of 1.01 Evangelion 2.0 06/27/2009 theatrical debut 05/26/2010 DVD/BD release of 2.22

[]:             Quote Post by Redtophat » Mon Nov 26, 2012 12:49 pm Yeah, i'm not holding myself till that BD comes. Camrip here I come.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 26, 2012 1:47 pm No point in keeping this open, is there? The announcements will happen when they happen. EDIT: Never mind. The desire for this topic is simply too strong.

[]: [NO SPOILERS] Striking while the iron is hot?             Quote Post by Toonsis » Wed Nov 28, 2012 12:24 am I'm new here. I've been a peeping tom on the site for a while, especially the forums. Probably around 2 years. I have no idea why I've taken so long to finally register. Anywho, I have a subject I'd like to touch on briefly regarding the U.S. release DVD/Blu-ray-wise of Evangelion 3.0 I've always wondered why there is such a massive delay between releases involving Japan and the U.S. And since I hardly know of any of the legalities revolving around licensing and such, I suppose I'm kind of clueless about the whole thing. But, is there some form of obstacle that prevents a company such as FUNimation from receiving the final product and dubbing it AFTER or during it's run through Japanese theaters but BEFORE the Japanese DVD/Blu-ray, and maybe releasing them around the same time? Seeing as how they have to dub it, I feel it'd be fair time-wise. It's not like they'd get the license and instantly release it with just the original Japanese audio track. I guess what story I'm trying to elaborate and then make short is: why does it seem impossible for a dubbing company to license said anime before it's released via DVD, dub it while it's in the wings and strike while the iron is hot and the hype for the new movie is still there instead of us waiting for a dubbed release 2+ years from now? I don't think I understand things I think I should. And I do apologize for rambling. Glad to be a part of these forums, now.

[]:             Quote Post by jblank » Wed Nov 28, 2012 12:30 am Reverse imports for one thing. Japanese otaku would buy the cheaper US DVD/BR release and cannibalize domestic Japanese sales.

[]:             Quote Post by Toonsis » Wed Nov 28, 2012 12:37 am That is true, but seeing how overwhelmingly anticipated this movie was to the Japanese audience, the ticket sales alone should diminsh the 'reverse import' thoughts. Is that subject really that big of an issue over there, or does it show that they are just really price inflated? I can't imagine how much of that money probably went directly into Hideaki's pocket due to the independent nature of these movies being produced.

[]:             Quote Post by jblank » Wed Nov 28, 2012 12:53 am licensing contracts can be really strict, and there may be a clause that mandates that any DVD release is later than the domestic Japanese one. the Japanese have always played hardball in that regard. even during the peak anime licensing boom of the mid-late 90s, America has always been seen as a secondary market.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Nov 28, 2012 8:24 am Toonsis wrote: Is that subject really that big of an issue over there, or does it show that they are just really price inflated?

[]:             Quote Post by jimmiel » Wed Nov 28, 2012 11:00 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: They're removing Japanese tracks from American BDs (Persona 4) or blocking overseas HD releases tout-a-court (Panty&Stocking), it looks pretty evident that it's considered a serious issue.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Wed Nov 28, 2012 2:46 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: They're removing Japanese tracks from American BDs (Persona 4)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Nov 28, 2012 4:52 pm Well, first off, an entire year of the wait is between the Japanese theatrical and Japanese home video release. The localizing distributor can't begin working on a domestic product until the Japanese equivalent exists. They can't work from a theatrical print, especially in the case where the theatrical version varies from the final home version. So in the case of Funimation, they have to bid, license, translate, cast, dub, mix, fabricate, QA, market, advertise, tour, ship, etc. Not to mention they arrange a theatrical run in there too. All of that takes time. That's where the second year comes from. Best case scenario, they skip the limited theatrical run and shave off... maybe 2-3 months? I want it as much as anyone else, but these things take time.

[]:             Quote Post by Toonsis » Wed Nov 28, 2012 11:41 pm They can't work from a theatrical print? I imagine that the finished work isn't strictly standardized to only a theatrical release, right? That would make it seem like they create this project without having a DVD release in mind for the future, no? I'm fairly certain you wouldn't know this information, but shouldn't the instant it's been inserted for play in Japanese theaters, the work on a DVD/Blu-ray date and release already be underway? Does it work that way? Or is there some specific reason aside from it going from 3.0 to 3.33 via DVD, that it takes over a year alone for Japan to get a home release? Again, I'm pretty much in the dark about the procedures. And I'm sorry for all of the questions. I'm actually just really curious, cosidering how long the whole process is. I also apologize for the use of the word 'release' 5 times.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Thu Nov 29, 2012 1:06 am Toonsis wrote: They can't work from a theatrical print? I imagine that the finished work isn't strictly standardized to only a theatrical release, right? That would make it seem like they create this project without having a DVD release in mind for the future, no? I'm fairly certain you wouldn't know this information, but shouldn't the instant it's been inserted for play in Japanese theaters, the work on a DVD/Blu-ray date and release already be underway? Does it work that way? Or is there some specific reason aside from it going from 3.0 to 3.33 via DVD, that it takes over a year alone for Japan to get a home release? Again, I'm pretty much in the dark about the procedures. And I'm sorry for all of the questions. I'm actually just really curious, cosidering how long the whole process is. I also apologize for the use of the word 'release' 5 times.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Nov 29, 2012 5:25 am The Eva Monkey wrote: I want it as much as anyone else, but these things take time.

[]:             Quote Post by Dan The Cat » Fri Nov 30, 2012 11:48 am Jayfive wrote: Testify! Given the things ive seen thus far, I want it to be given the treatment it deserves. The best I can hope for in the UK is a showing of the sub at some film festival or other. Thats what happened with 2.22. Anime is very much a marginal thing in the UK compared to other foreign markets like the US and Australia so we wont even get a limited theatrical run.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Nov 30, 2012 3:30 pm Yeah, that was the one I saw too late to attend :)
